message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Melvyn's Restaurant (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Continental / classic supper club
Why it surfaced in discovery: Opened 1975 at the Ingleside Inn by Mel Haber; celebrated 50th anniversary in 2025. Frank Sinatra held his 1976 rehearsal dinner here; featured in the Netflix documentary 'Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs.' Still serves tableside steak Diane (Sinatra's favorite). Tennis Club neighborhood, near downtown.
Discovery sources: https://inglesideestate.com/melvyns-50th-anniversary/ | https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/melvyns-at-50-a-toast-to-glamour-legacy-and-the-man-who-made-palm-springs-shine/ | https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/frank-sinatra-remember-by-mel-haber-at-melvyns-in-palm-springs/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

input.prompt: Extract information about Melvyn's Restaurant: history, ownership, awards, recognition, menu, hours, and any structured data signals.

input.query: Melvyn's Restaurant Palm Springs reviews Google Yelp

content.content:
# Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar - Information Summary

## History & Ownership
Melvyn's opened in **1975** at the historic Ingleside Estate under the vision of **Mel Haber**. The restaurant was established to restore the estate's elegant charm and create a fine dining destination.

## Celebrity Legacy
The venue became famous as a Hollywood haunt. Notable patrons included "Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando, Marilyn Monroe, Elizabeth Taylor" and members of the Rat Pack. A particularly significant moment: Sinatra held his wedding rehearsal dinner there in 1976.

## Character & Ambiance
The restaurant embodies classic supper-club aesthetics with "chandeliers, velvet seating, live music, and tableside service." It's described as a "living time capsule" preserving Old Hollywood glamour through contemporary hospitality.

## 50th Anniversary Celebration
Running from October 13 through November 15, 2025, the restaurant celebrated five decades with era-inspired menus, period music, and a grand finale event featuring "live music, nostalgic entertainment, and an evening of open-bar cocktails."

## Operating Hours
- **Dinner:** 5pm-9pm Daily
- **Bar Service:** 11am-10pm Daily
- **Live Music:** 6pm-10pm Daily

## Contact Information
- **Phone:** (760) 325-2323
- **Location:** 200 W Ramon Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92264
- **Instagram:** @melvynsrestaurant

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Melvyn's Restaurant Palm Springs reviews:

## Overview
Located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, Melvyn's is elegantly upbeat and the home of authentic Hollywood glamour.

## Google & Yelp Reviews
The restaurant has 968 reviews on Yelp, with reviews showing mixed feedback. Positive reviews praise "Great service, drinks and food" and note that "If you want old school vibe, mosey to Melvins." 

However, some negative reviews criticize pricing and service quality, with comments about portions being very small and very overpriced, with the $65 fish special being about half a typical portion size and far too overcooked and dry.

## OpenTable Reviews
On OpenTable, the restaurant has 4876 reviews with positive feedback like "Fantastic meal, service and space. Would highly recommend and can't wait to go back".

## General Reputation
Melvyn's offers old Hollywood glamour and timeless luxury, with classic American fare and cocktails in a space once a private hideaway for stars like Marlon Brando, Frank Sinatra, and Marilyn Monroe.

# Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar - Schema & Site Quality Analysis

## Schema.org Markup
The page **lacks explicit schema.org markup** in the provided content. While the HTML contains rich information, there's no visible JSON-LD or microdata structured data implementation for Restaurant, LocalBusiness, or other schema types.

## Agent-Readable Signals

**Present:**
- Clear hours (Dinner: 5pm–9pm, Bar: 11am–10pm, Live Music: 6pm–10pm daily)
- Full address (200 W Ramon Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92264)
- Phone numbers ((760) 325-2323 for Melvyn's; (760) 325-0046 for hotel)
- Email contact
- Menu reference (with accessible menu option)
- Reservation system (OpenTable link provided)
- Pool service details (8am–3pm daily)

**Missing:**
- No explicit cuisine type declaration
- No price range indicator
- No service type schema (dine-in, takeout, delivery status)
- No seating capacity information

## Site Quality & Awards

**Professional presentation:** Comprehensive FAQ, multiple navigation paths, Instagram integration, high-quality imagery

**Notable accolades:**
- "2023 Wine Spectator Award"
- Featured in Infatuation LA's "25 Best Places To Eat & Drink in Palm Springs"
- Esquire's "Twenty-Seven Best Bars in America"
- "The classic Melvyn's is a time capsule" (Skift)

**Overall:** Strong brand positioning with established credibility, though technical SEO could be enhanced with proper schema implementation.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining options in Palm Springs, particularly those with a supper club and continental style:

## Top Fine Dining Supper Club & Continental Options:

**The Purple Room** - The sophistication and glamour of the Rat Pack era comes alive at Michael Holmes' Purple Room, the desert's premier supper club. The Purple Room is where Frank, Sammy, Dean and their pals cavorted on and off the stage in the swinging 60s, offering an intimate and inviting room, classic cocktails, world-class cuisine and the best in live entertainment seven nights a week. The Purple Room has been voted #1 on TripAdvisor and one of the top 100 restaurants in the country by Open Table.

**Miro's Restaurant** - Miro's Restaurant in Palm Springs is a true gem for Mediterranean cuisine enthusiasts, celebrated as the best Mediterranean-Continental fine dining experience in the area.

**Vicky's of Santa Fe** - An adobe-style restaurant established in 1989, with an old-school piano bar and lounge entertainment that keep the nostalgic supper club era alive.

**Continental Restaurants:**
- Zin American Bistro stands out as a beacon of Continental cuisine with over 20 years of serving locals and visitors, with standout dishes like lobster pasta and short rib pappardelle.

- Spencer's Restaurant, set at the historic Palm Springs Tennis Club, is synonymous with the desert's classic style, offering dishes like shrimp cocktail, Chilean sea bass, and filet mignon.

I've completed your web search. Here are the results for "Melvyn's Palm Springs best restaurants Coachella Valley date night":

## Key Findings:

**Melvyn's Restaurant Overview:**
Melvyn's at Ingleside Estate is a legendary restaurant and lounge featuring old Hollywood glamour and timeless luxury. The restaurant offers a modern California twist on classic American fare and cocktails in a space that was once a private hideaway for stars like Marlon Brando, Frank Sinatra, and Marilyn Monroe.

**Dining Experience:**
Melvyn's serves refined takes on classic American fare, including steaks, fresh seafood, elegant pastas, and nostalgic sides, paired with an extensive wine list and craft cocktail offerings. The lounge offers a vintage-inspired setting with a full bar menu, seasonal cocktails, and nightly live piano.

**For Date Night:**
For a balance of atmosphere and quieter dining, choose an early weekday evening (Mon–Thu) or Sunday afternoon/evening; if you want the piano-bar energy, go Friday or Saturday night. The restaurant offers patio, lanai and garden-room seating with lovely outdoor areas that offer a quieter or garden-like experience.

**Reservations:**
Reservations are highly encouraged, especially for dinner and live music evenings, and can be reserved directly via OpenTable.

The search results confirm Melvyn's is considered one of the best fine dining options in the Coachella Valley, particularly popular for romantic date night experiences.

Links: [{"title":"10 Steakhouses Beloved By Old Hollywood Stars - Tasting Table","url":"https://www.tastingtable.com/2024790/steakhouses-old-hollywood-stars-loved/"},{"title":"The Desert Lounge Where Old Hollywood Lives - PUNCH","url":"https://punchdrink.com/articles/the-desert-lounge-where-old-hollywood-lives/"},{"title":"This Old-School California Restaurant Was One Of Sinatra's Favorite Spots","url":"https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/celebrity/articles/old-school-california-restaurant-one-100300546.html"},{"title":"This Old-School California Restaurant Was One Of Sinatra's Favorite Spots - Mashed","url":"https://www.mashed.com/1956106/frank-sinatra-melvyns-diane-steak-california-local/"},{"title":"12 Restaurants Frank Sinatra Loved Across The US - Tasting Table","url":"https://www.tastingtable.com/1517862/frank-sinatra-favorite-restaurants-united-states/"},{"title":"12 Old Hollywood Stars' Favorite Ways To Eat Steak - Tasting Table","url":"https://www.tastingtable.com/1690291/old-hollywood-stars-favorite-ways-steak/"},{"title":"Best Restaurants to Take Visitors in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/best-restaurants-visitors/"}]

Based on my search results, I found excellent information about classic Old Hollywood restaurants in the Coachella Valley with connections to Sinatra and steak Diane:

## Melvyn's Restaurant (Coachella Valley)

The Ingleside Inn, located at the end of West Ramon Road in Palm Springs (in the Coachella Valley), has been operating for 90 years, with its restaurant and bar called Melvyn's. Melvyn's delivers steak Diane and other classic dishes to regulars in an Old Hollywood setting.

Located at the Ingleside Inn, Melvyn's was one of Frank Sinatra's favorite restaurants, opened in September 1975 by Mel Haber. Sinatra was a regular, choosing a cozy corner booth near the door where he could sit back and sip a Jack Daniel's with water.

Sinatra's go-to orders included veal with vegetables and the now-famous Steak Diane, which came with a splendidly retro tableside presentation, complete with a roaring flame of cognac sauce. Steak Diane contains mushrooms, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, and cognac.

Melvyn's remains a stylish destination today, offering upscale American fare, cocktails, and live music, with Sinatra's beloved tableside-flambéed Steak Diane still on the menu. Melvyn's is one of the few restaurants in Southern California that still offers tableside service in its formal dining room, with the "show" when you order steak Diane.

input.name: Melvyn's Restaurant
input.cuisine_or_type: Continental / classic supper club (American with tableside service)
input.corridor: Downtown Palm Springs (Tennis Club neighborhood, Ingleside Estate)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~968 reviews; Tripadvisor ~976 reviews; OpenTable 4000+ reviews (combined: 5000+)
reputation.longevity: 50 years (opened 1975 by Mel Haber); celebrated 50th anniversary Oct-Nov 2025; located at Ingleside Estate (Ingleside Inn) which has operated as a hotel property for decades
credentials: Wine Spectator Award (2023, per restaurant site)
Featured in Esquire's 'Best Bars in America' (per restaurant site) [UNVERIFIED year]
Featured in The Infatuation LA's '25 Best Places To Eat & Drink in Palm Springs'
Featured in Netflix documentary 'Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs'
OpenTable Diners' Choice listings (romantic / live music / fine dining categories)
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 6-12 months) lean positive, frequently praising the Old Hollywood atmosphere, live piano, tableside steak Diane, and the 50th anniversary programming. Negative reviews cluster around high prices, occasionally inconsistent food execution (one cited $65 fish special as small/overcooked), and uneven service on busy nights.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'classic Old Hollywood restaurants Coachella Valley', 'Sinatra steak Diane Palm Springs', 'supper club Palm Springs', 'date night Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Springs'. Less consistent for generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' where newer/El Paseo venues dominate.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when surfaced — agents correctly cite 1975 opening, Mel Haber, Sinatra association, tableside steak Diane, Ingleside Estate location, and supper-club character. No major hallucinations observed in surfaced descriptions.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Website (inglesideestate.com/melvyns/) presents Melvyn's as a sub-page of the parent estate site rather than a standalone restaurant property. Hours, address, phone, and OpenTable reservation link are present and clean, but no visible JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant or LocalBusiness markup was detected. No explicit cuisine type, price range, or service-type schema. Menu is reachable but not structured for agents. Strong narrative content and imagery; weak technical SEO for AI ingestion.

competitors: The Purple Room (Palm Springs) — direct supper-club competitor, also Rat Pack heritage
Spencer's Restaurant (Palm Springs Tennis Club) — adjacent neighborhood, classic upscale
Mr. Lyons Steakhouse (downtown Palm Springs) — classic steakhouse vibe
Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs) — fine-dining peer in downtown PS
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — heritage anchor for the Tennis Club / Ingleside Estate sub-corridor and a recognized anchor for the Palm Springs supper-club / Old Hollywood category
query_wins: Sinatra-related queries (steak Diane, Sinatra's favorite Palm Springs restaurant)
Old Hollywood / supper club queries for Palm Springs

convertibility.ownership_signal: hotel-restaurant (operated as the restaurant of the Ingleside Estate / Ingleside Inn). Mel Haber founded both; ownership has reportedly transitioned since Haber's passing but current ownership structure not fully verified here.
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real, well-produced parent website (inglesideestate.com) with professional photography, active Instagram (@melvynsrestaurant), paid OpenTable presence, coverage in Visit Palm Springs editorial, and a months-long 50th anniversary campaign in 2025 indicating real marketing investment. No agency footprint confirmed.
convertibility.reasoning: Melvyn's sits between tiers. It is tied to the Ingleside Estate hotel property, which makes it functionally a hotel-restaurant — that often makes reaching an independent DM harder and pushes it toward 'anchor-reference.' However, it is an independently branded local institution (not a chain or franchise), runs its own social and a 50th anniversary campaign, and has the marketing footprint of a real convertible operator. Without confirming who controls the marketing decision today (estate owner vs. restaurant GM), the right tier is 'unclear' pending an ownership/decision-maker check.
